description_long: This object is a tall, rectangular stone container constructed from irregularly shaped, weathered stones stacked and mortared together. The exterior surface is rough and uneven, with visible crevices and a natural, aged patina, including some faint moss or lichen growth in the joints. The top is open, revealing a hollow interior, suggesting it functions as a receptacle or planter. The stonework is consistent on all sides, with subtle variations in stone size and coloration, giving it an authentic, rustic appearance. The corners are slightly rounded and the edges are not perfectly straight, emphasizing its hand-built, traditional masonry style. The coloration is predominantly a mix of light tan, gray, and hints of green, typical of aged stone exposed to the elements.
description: A tall, open-topped rectangular container made of rough, weathered stone blocks with a rustic, aged appearance and visible moss in the joints.
description_view_0: Front view shows the open top and the uneven, stacked stone surface with visible mortar and some moss.
description_view_1: This side displays a broader face of the stonework, highlighting the irregular stone pattern and subtle color variations.
description_view_2: A narrower side view, emphasizing the vertical alignment of the stones and the slightly rounded corners.
description_view_3: Opposite broad face, similar to view 1, with more visible weathering and a consistent stone pattern.
attribute_location_context: garden landscaping or urban park planter
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked or mortared in place on a level surface
attribute_weathering_pattern: Visible moss growth in some joints and a generally aged, weathered surface with slight discoloration.
